info.title: UiPath Command Line Interface
info.description: The official UiPath Command Line Interface for automating RPA workflows, managing Orchestrator deployments, and packaging automation projects.

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Check project(s) for workflow analyzer violations.

options.name: --analyzerTraceLevel
options.description: Override severity for all analyzer rules. Allowed: Off, Error, Warning, Info, Verbose. Default: Error.

options.name: --captureCommandToJsonFile
options.description:
(Do not use this option for production purposes) Absolute path to a JSON file where the command will be captured instead of being executed.
The resulted JSON file can be used to execute the captured command with `uipcli run`.
Useful to understand what the `arguments JSON file` should contain, when executing `uipcli run`.

options.name: --disableBuiltInNugetFeeds
options.description: Disable built in nuget feeds.

options.name: --disableTelemetry
options.description: Disable telemetry data.

options.name: --governanceFilePath
options.description: Pass governance policies containing the Workflow Analyzer rules. Policies can be downloaded from Automation Ops, or extracted from the exported compressed zip file from Studio. For details, please consult the documentation.

options.name: --identityUrl
options.description: Url of your identity server. This is only required for PaaS deployments.

options.name: --ignoredRules
options.description: A comma-separated list of rules to be ignored by the analysis procedure.

options.name: --language
options.description: Language for the translated logs.

options.name: --nugetConfigFilePath
options.description: Path to the NuGet.Config file containing feeds.

options.name: --orchestratorAccountForApp
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with id, secret and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --orchestratorAccountName
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with the refresh token and client id.

options.name: --orchestratorApplicationId
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The external application id. Must be used together with organization name, secret and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --orchestratorApplicationScope
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The space-separated list of application scopes. Must be used together with organization name, id and secret for external application.

options.name: --orchestratorApplicationSecret
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The external application secret. Must be used together with organization name, id and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --orchestratorAuthToken
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator refresh token used for authentication. Must be used together with the account name and client id.

options.name: --orchestratorFolder
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator folder name.

options.name: --orchestratorPassword
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator password used for authentication. Must be used together with the username.

options.name: --orchestratorTenant
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator tenant.

options.name: --orchestratorUrl
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator URL.

options.name: --orchestratorUsername
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator username used for authentication. Must be used together with the password.

options.name: --resultPath
options.description: The full path to a JSON file where the result json file will be created. Otherwise print it to the standard console.

options.name: --stopOnRuleViolation
options.description: Fail when any rule is violated.

options.name: --traceLevel
options.description: Specifies what types of log messages to output  One of the following values: None, Critical, Error, Warning, Information, Verbose. (default Information)

options.name: --treatWarningsAsErrors
options.description: Treat warnings as errors.

arguments.name: Workspace Path
arguments.description: Path to the folder where to search for a solution file or RPA projects.

commands.name: args
commands.description: Execute as specified in the arguments in args file

options.name: --argsFile
options.description: The path to the solution file.

commands.name: asset
commands.description: Manage Orchestrator assets.

commands.name: build
commands.description: Compile the given project.

options.name: --output
options.description: The directory where the build will generated

options.name: --project
options.description: The path to the project directory

options.name: --additional-rules-folder
options.description: Optional addititional folder to use for scanning analyzer rules

options.name: --build-version
options.description: The build version

options.name: --default-severity
options.description: Severity override for analyzer rules

options.name: --detailed
options.description: Generate detailed results in json format (use in automated tests)

options.name: --disable-built-in-nuget-feeds
options.description: Disables the built-in NuGet feeds

options.name: --exclude-configured-sources
options.description: Excludes the configured sources at the user and machine level

options.name: --fetch-metadata
options.description: Fetch activity metadata

options.name: --file-handle-constrained
options.description: Enable file handle conservation mode

options.name: --format-logs
options.description: Format logs as json

options.name: --include-metadata
options.description: Include metadata in the build output

options.name: --include-sources
options.description: Include sources in package

options.name: --log-file
options.description: not yet implemented

options.name: --log-level
options.description: The log level

options.name: --no-optimize-deps
options.description: Include runtime dependencies in the build output

options.name: --nuget-config-file
options.description: The nuget config file

options.name: --output-type
options.description: The output type of the project

options.name: --pack-options
options.description: Generate a nupkg package using the specified options.

options.name: --pack-options-file
options.description: Generate a nupkg package using the specified options file.

options.name: --package-name
options.description: The package name

options.name: --policy-file
options.description: Path to policy file

options.name: --policy-file-type
options.description: Rules config file format: Default, AutomationOps, Studio

options.name: --skip-analyze
options.description: Skip the analyze step

options.name: --skip-validate
options.description: Skip the validation step

options.name: --split-compilation
options.description: Enable split compilation to create a separate assembly for each workflow. This also enables caching, will update assemblies only if workflows changed.

options.name: --split-packages
options.description: Enable split runtime and design time packages.

commands.name: delete
commands.description: Delete assets from an Orchestrator instance (based on asset name).

options.name: --accountName
options.description: The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with the refresh token and client id.

options.name: --applicationId
options.description: The external application id. Must be used together with organization name, secret and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --applicationSecret
options.description: The external application secret. Must be used together with organization name, id and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --password
options.description: The Orchestrator password used for authentication. Must be used together with the username.

options.name: --token
options.description: The Orchestrator refresh token used for authentication. Must be used together with the account name and client id.

options.name: --username
options.description: The Orchestrator username used for authentication. Must be used together with the password.

options.name: --accountForApp
options.description: The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with id, secret and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --applicationScope
options.description: The space-separated list of application scopes. Must be used together with organization name, id and secret for external application.

options.name: --captureCommandToJsonFile
options.description:
(Do not use this option for production purposes) Absolute path to a JSON file where the command will be captured instead of being executed.
The resulted JSON file can be used to execute the captured command with `uipcli run`.
Useful to understand what the `arguments JSON file` should contain, when executing `uipcli run`.

options.name: --disableTelemetry
options.description: Disable telemetry data.

options.name: --identityUrl
options.description: Url of your identity server. This is only required for PaaS deployments.

options.name: --language
options.description: Language for the translated logs.

options.name: --organizationUnit
options.description: The Orchestrator folder name.

options.name: --traceLevel
options.description: Specifies what types of log messages to output  One of the following values: None, Critical, Error, Warning, Information, Verbose. (default is Information)

arguments.name: Assets File
arguments.description:
The file containing the assets to be deleted.

The following is a sample csv file. The column names are required! Only the first column is used but you need to at least have empty columns in place.
name,type,value
asset_1_name,,asset_value # we can have comments
asset_2_name,integer,
asset_3_name,,
asset_4_name,credential,username:password

arguments.name: Orchestrator URL
arguments.description: The URL of the Orchestrator instance.

arguments.name: Orchestrator Tenant
arguments.description: The tenant of the Orchestrator instance.

commands.name: deploy
commands.description: Deploy packages to an Orchestrator instance, optionally publishing them to a set of environments.

options.name: --accountName
options.description: The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with the refresh token and client id.

options.name: --applicationId
options.description: The external application id. Must be used together with organization name, secret and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --applicationSecret
options.description: The external application secret. Must be used together with organization name, id and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --password
options.description: The Orchestrator password used for authentication. Must be used together with the username.

options.name: --token
options.description: The Orchestrator refresh token used for authentication. Must be used together with the account name and client id.

options.name: --username
options.description: The Orchestrator username used for authentication. Must be used together with the password.

options.name: --accountForApp
options.description: The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with id, secret and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --applicationScope
options.description: The space-separated list of application scopes. Must be used together with organization name, id and secret for external application.

options.name: --captureCommandToJsonFile
options.description:
(Do not use this option for production purposes) Absolute path to a JSON file where the command will be captured instead of being executed.
The resulted JSON file can be used to execute the captured command with `uipcli run`.
Useful to understand what the `arguments JSON file` should contain, when executing `uipcli run`.

options.name: --createProcess
options.description: When set to true, it will create a process in the respective folder as per the given entry points. (default true)

options.name: --disableTelemetry
options.description: Disable telemetry data.

options.name: --entryPointsPath
options.description: Define the specific entry points to create or update a process. This is the filePath of the entry point starting from the root of the project. For classic folders only one entry point can be specified, for each environment it will be created or updated a process with the specified entry point.

options.name: --environments
options.description: (Only for classic folders) The comma-separated list of environments to deploy the package to. If the environment does not belong to the default folder (organization unit) it must be prefixed with the folder name, e.g. AccountingTeam\TestEnvironment

options.name: --identityUrl
options.description: Url of your identity server. This is only required for PaaS deployments.

options.name: --ignoreLibraryDeployConflict
options.description: Ignore the conflict when deploying a library package with the same version as an existing one. By default, the deployment will fail if a library with the same version already exists in the Orchestrator instance.

options.name: --language
options.description: Language for the translated logs.

options.name: --organizationUnit
options.description: The Orchestrator folder name.

options.name: --processDescription
options.description: Set the process description to the specified value. Takes highest priority over any other description source.

options.name: --processName
options.description: Optionally provide a custom name for the process to be updated/created

options.name: --processNames
options.description:
Optionally provide a .csv file with custom names for the processes to be created/updated.
The following is a sample csv file. The column names are required!
PackageName,EntryPointName,ProcessName
MyPackage,Main.xaml,MyProcess
YetAnotherPackage,CustomEntryPoint.xaml,YetAnotherProcess

options.name: --traceLevel
options.description: Specifies what types of log messages to output  One of the following values: None, Critical, Error, Warning, Information, Verbose. (default is Information)

options.name: --use-package-description
options.description: Use the package description as the process description. On create this is the default behavior. On update this overrides the existing process description with the package description.

arguments.name: Package(s) Path
arguments.description: The path to a folder containing packages, or to a package file.

arguments.name: Orchestrator URL
arguments.description: The URL of the Orchestrator instance.

arguments.name: Orchestrator Tenant
arguments.description: The tenant of the Orchestrator instance.

options.description: Pass governance policies containing the Workflow Analyzer rules. Policies can be downloaded from Automation Ops, or extracted from the exported compressed zip file from Studio. For details, please consult the documentation.

options.name: --libraryIdentityUrl
options.description: Url of your identity server. This is only required for PaaS deployments.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorAccountForApp
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with id, secret and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorAccountName
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with the refresh token and client id.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorApplicationId
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The external application id. Must be used together with organization name, secret and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorApplicationScope
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The space-separated list of application scopes. Must be used together with organization name, id and secret for external application.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorApplicationSecret
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The external application secret. Must be used together with organization name, id and scope(s) for external application.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorAuthToken
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator refresh token used for authentication. Must be used together with the organization name and client id.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorFolder
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator folder name.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorPassword
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator password used for authentication. Must be used together with the username.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorTenant
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator tenant.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorUrl
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator URL.

options.name: --libraryOrchestratorUsername
options.description: (Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator username used for authentication. Must be used together with the password.

options.name: --nugetConfigFilePath
options.description: Path to the NuGet.Config file containing feeds.

options.name: --outputType
options.description: Force the output to a specific type.

options.name: --projectUrl
options.description: Automation Hub idea URL.

options.name: --releaseNotes
options.description: Add release notes.

options.name: --repositoryBranch
options.description: Repository branch where the project was built from.

options.name: --repositoryCommit
options.description: Repository commit where the project was built from.

options.name: --repositoryType
options.description: VCS system repository type.

options.name: --repositoryUrl
options.description: Repository url where project is versioned.

options.name: --splitOutput
options.description: Enables the output split to runtime and design libraries.

Useful to understand what the `arguments JSON file` should contain, when executing `uipcli run`.
